zh.lifeSummary: William de Veteri-Ponte of Langton, Carriden & Bolton是William de Veteri-Ponte的兒子，於從大約1248年到1308年出生。他於大約1263年與Petronilla de Veteri Ponte在蘇格蘭結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: William de Veteri-Ponte of Langton, Carriden & Bolton是William de Veteri-Ponte的儿子，于从大约1248年到1308年出生。他于大约1263年与Petronilla de Veteri Ponte在苏格兰结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Sung, Jung, Yong, Chang, Kwang, Sang, Dong, Hyun, Jae, Jin, Kyung, Jong, Chong, Chung, Byung, Jeong, Pyong, Myong, Hak, Jin Hwan, Sook, Yeong, Choon.
Korean: variant of   O  .
Japanese: variant of Ō (see O ).

variants.name: O
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Bo Young, Chae, Chong Hwan, Dong Kun, Ho, Hwan, Hyun, Jong, Jung, Ki Soon, Kwang.  Korean Min, Chong, Chol, Jung Ho, Myong, Sa, Sung Kyun.

Korean (오; Chinese character: 吳): historically, there were two Chinese characters for the O surname, but by the late Koryŏ period (14th century AD ), one of them had disappeared. Some records indicate that there are as many as 210 different clans for the remaining character, but only sixteen can be positively documented. All sixteen of these clans originate from the same ancestor, O Ch’ŏm (吳 瞻), who migrated to Korea from China during the reign of Shilla's King Chijŭng (500–514 AD ). O is a common surname throughout Korea. Compare Oh .

nameMeanings.name: Langton,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from one or more of the many places called with Old English lang ‘long’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’, such as Langton Hall in Kirkby in Ashfield (Nottinghamshire), Langton Lodge in Hendon (Middlesex), Langton (Yorkshire), East and West Langton (Leicestershire), Langton by Horncastle, Langton by Spilsby, and Langton by Wragby (all in Lincolnshire), and Lanton (Northumb). Compare Lankton , Lanton , and Longton .

variants.name: Lanton
meanings: English: variant of   Langton  .

variants.name: Lankton
meanings: English: variant of   Langton  .
History: Thomas Lankton, born c. 1714 in Hartford County, CT, died 1783 in Litchfield County, CT, was a son of Joseph Langton.

variants.name: Longton
meanings: habitational name from Longton in Lancashire. The placename derives from Old English lang ‘long’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’.
variant of   Langton  .
English:

nameMeanings.name: Bolton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of numerous places in northern England named Bolton (Lancashire, Northumberland, Westmorland, and Yorkshire) or from Boulton in Derbyshire and East Lothian in Scotland, from Old English bothl ‘dwelling, house’ (see Bold 1) + tūn ‘enclosure, settlement’.

variants.name: Boulton
meanings: English: variant of   Bolton  .

variants.name: Bold
meanings: English (Lancashire and Staffordshire): from Middle English bold ‘dwelling-house’ (Old English bold the West Midland and northwestern form of Old English bōthl, bōtl), especially in the sense ‘castle or manor house’ for someone who lived or worked at the main house, or a habitational name from one of the many places called with this word, for example Bold (Lancashire), The Bold in Aston Botterell (Shropshire), Booden Farm in Haughton, Booth in Blithfield, and Bull Bridge in Penkridge (all Staffordshire). Compare Bodle .
English: nickname from Middle English bold, Old English b(e)ald ‘bold, brave, noble’, which in Middle English developed the additional sense ‘arrogant, impudent, shameless’.
English: variant of   Bolt   and possibly a variant of   Bald   2.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: William de Veteri-Ponte of Langton, Carriden & Bolton was born from about 1248 to 1308 as the son of William de Veteri-Ponte. He married Petronilla de Veteri Ponte about 1263, in Scotland. They were the parents of at least 1 son.

fr.lifeSummary: William de Veteri-Ponte of Langton, Carriden & Bolton est né du environ 1248 au 1308, fils de William de Veteri-Ponte. Il a épousé Petronilla de Veteri Ponte environ 1263, à Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils.

de.lifeSummary: William de Veteri-Ponte of Langton, Carriden & Bolton wurde von um 1248 bis 1308, als Sohn von William de Veteri-Ponte geboren. Er hat um 1263 in Scotland Petronilla de Veteri Ponte geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn.

it.lifeSummary: William de Veteri-Ponte of Langton, Carriden & Bolton è nato il da circa 1248 a 1308 come figlio di William de Veteri-Ponte. Lui sposato Petronilla de Veteri Ponte circa 1263, nel Scotland. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio.

ja.lifeSummary: William de Veteri-Ponte of Langton, Carriden & Boltonはから約1248年まで1308年にWilliam de Veteri-Ponteの息子として生まれた。約1263年にスコットランドでPetronilla de Veteri Ponteと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。

ko.lifeSummary: William de Veteri-Ponte of Langton, Carriden & Bolton(은)는 대략 1248년 부터 1308년 까지 William de Veteri-Ponte의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 대략 1263년 스컷랜드에서 Petronilla de Veteri Ponte(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: William de Veteri-Ponte of Langton, Carriden & Bolton nasceu de aproximadamente 1248 a 1308 filho de William de Veteri-Ponte. Ele casou-se com Petronilla de Veteri Ponte aproximadamente 1263, em Scotland. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho.

ru.lifeSummary: William de Veteri-Ponte of Langton, Carriden & Bolton родился с около 1248 по 1308. Его отца звали William de Veteri-Ponte. Он женился на женщине по имени Petronilla de Veteri Ponte около 1263 г. в местности Шотландия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын.
